prompt: Add the bat command line tool to my Homebrew packages as the plain string "bat" only, with no inline comments.

provider.note: The key value is never written to this report. Failures may reflect provider billing/auth state.

catalog.label: Homebrew bat package add
catalog.source: Current Computer Use E2E default; maps to eval package/homebrew install coverage.
catalog.note: Runs prompt -> Review -> Summary/Diff -> Build boundary -> Step 3 Commit -> History rollback cleanup.

catalog.label: Screenshot defaults review-only calibration
catalog.source: WDIO fixture basicPromptsConfigureScreenshots and eval CSV case 33 / golden-set system-defaults coverage.
catalog.note: Optional calibration lane. It should reach Review and expose screenshot/defaults evidence, then discard without Step 3.

catalog.label: Protected flake input adversarial boundary
catalog.source: Eval spreadsheet protected-file family (#121/#208/#210). Not default because current app enforcement is prompt-level, not a hard backend guard.
catalog.note: Keep out of the default PR lane until nixmac has hard protected-file enforcement and a reliable refusal signal.

evolvedCaseStrategy.reviewDecision: Claude review kept homebrew-bat as the only default full-lifecycle PR case, moved screenshots-defaults to optional calibration, and moved protected-flake-input to an adversarial advisory lane until hard backend enforcement exists.

safety.note: Discard/build confirmation is only allowed when disposable config mode is explicitly proven.

prFocus.title: Add Computer Use E2E non-blocking PR report

cleanup.note: Remote app state was not restored by this runner. CI wrapper is responsible for remote app-support backup/restore; local artifacts are retained.

claims.claim: App launches and first screen is usable
claims.evidence: Computer Use saw the nixmac app window, prompt surface, progress stepper, and top-level controls.

claims.claim: Update banner does not block the main workflow
claims.evidence: No dismissible update banner was visible; no banner blocked the main workflow.

claims.claim: Settings General tab visibly renders
claims.evidence: Settings opened and General-related content was visible.

claims.claim: Settings AI Models tab visibly renders
claims.evidence: AI Models settings content was visible with provider/model controls.

claims.claim: Settings API Keys tab visibly renders
claims.evidence: API Keys content was visible after Computer Use polling, including OpenRouter/API key controls.

claims.claim: Settings Preferences tab visibly renders
claims.evidence: Preferences settings content was visible with confirmation controls.

claims.claim: My History opens and renders
claims.evidence: History rendered a visible state.

claims.claim: Console opens and renders
claims.evidence: Console rendered visible content.

claims.claim: Give Feedback opens and can be cancelled
claims.evidence: Feedback dialog rendered and no submission was made.

claims.claim: Report Issue opens and can be cancelled
claims.evidence: Report Issue dialog rendered and no submission was made.

claims.claim: Home suggestion cards are visible/clickable
claims.evidence: A suggestion card was clicked and the UI remained usable.

claims.claim: A typed real intent can be submitted
claims.evidence: Computer Use entered the real prompt into the app prompt field.

claims.claim: Real provider workflow reaches Review
claims.evidence: The prompt was submitted, but Review did not appear before the polling window ended.

claims.claim: Summary tab renders after intent review
claims.evidence: Summary was not tested because the real provider workflow did not reach Review.

claims.claim: Diff tab renders after intent review
claims.evidence: Diff was not tested because the real provider workflow did not reach Review.

claims.claim: Build & Test destructive boundary appears before activation
claims.evidence: Build & Test boundary was not tested because the real provider workflow did not reach Review.

claims.claim: Step 3 Save / Keep changes persists a change
claims.evidence: Step 3 Save / Keep changes was not tested because the real provider workflow did not reach Review.

claims.claim: Rollback cleanup returns disposable config to clean state
claims.evidence: Rollback cleanup was not tested because the real provider workflow did not reach Review.

claims.claim: Discard confirmation is guarded and only confirmed in disposable state
claims.evidence: Discard-after-review was not tested because the real provider workflow did not reach Review.

claims.claim: Core UX/UI surfaces are captured and inspectable
claims.evidence: Screenshots/text snapshots cover the core app shell, settings, support dialogs, prompt input, and provider flow.

claims.claim: Scenario results include inspectable visual/text evidence
claims.evidence: Every passing scenario has a linked screenshot or redacted accessibility text artifact in the proof catalog.

claims.claim: Main branch user-visible coverage stays mapped
claims.evidence: Coverage manifest v1 maps 13 user-visible surfaces to scenarios or explicit waivers. Explicit waivers: homebrew-badge: Not yet deterministic in the remote major-feature lane; needs a fixture with Homebrew installed and a known untracked tap/brew/cask diff before the suite can honestly claim coverage for the badge popover and Add to config action. | onboarding-permissions: Current remote lane starts from a configured app state. This must become a dedicated clean-profile Computer Use scenario before the suite can honestly claim full main coverage. | sudo-local-activation-fixture: The DXU remote lane disables sudo_local in the disposable fixture because unattended MacinCloud AppleScript activation can hang while creating /etc/pam.d/sudo_local. This is an explicit CI fixture waiver, not proof that the production sudo_local path works. | preview-indicator: Not yet in the remote major-feature lane; needs either a deterministic preview-state setup or a real workflow that activates it.

claims.claim: PR-specific user-visible behavior is covered when applicable
claims.evidence: User-visible changed files mapped to scenarios, but PR-focused scenarios did not all complete: Real provider workflow reaches Review, Summary tab renders after intent review, Diff tab renders after intent review, Build & Test destructive boundary appears before activation, Step 3 Save / Keep changes persists a change, Rollback cleanup returns disposable config to clean state

claims.claim: Generated HTML report is inspected with Computer Use
claims.evidence: Computer Use inspected the generated report in Safari; report text and evidence sections were visible.

mainCoverageFreshness.label: Main branch user-visible coverage stays mapped
notes: Coverage manifest v1 maps 13 user-visible surfaces to scenarios or explicit waivers. Explicit waivers: homebrew-badge: Not yet deterministic in the remote major-feature lane; needs a fixture with Homebrew installed and a known untracked tap/brew/cask diff before the suite can honestly claim coverage for the badge popover and Add to config action. | onboarding-permissions: Current remote lane starts from a configured app state. This must become a dedicated clean-profile Computer Use scenario before the suite can honestly claim full main coverage. | sudo-local-activation-fixture: The DXU remote lane disables sudo_local in the disposable fixture because unattended MacinCloud AppleScript activation can hang while creating /etc/pam.d/sudo_local. This is an explicit CI fixture waiver, not proof that the production sudo_local path works. | preview-indicator: Not yet in the remote major-feature lane; needs either a deterministic preview-state setup or a real workflow that activates it.
mainCoverageFreshness.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
mainCoverageFreshness.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.

prSpecificCoverage.label: PR-specific user-visible behavior is covered when applicable
notes: User-visible changed files mapped to scenarios, but PR-focused scenarios did not all complete: Real provider workflow reaches Review, Summary tab renders after intent review, Diff tab renders after intent review, Build & Test destructive boundary appears before activation, Step 3 Save / Keep changes persists a change, Rollback cleanup returns disposable config to clean state

prSpecificCoverage.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.

reportInspection.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
reportInspection.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.

processEnvVerification.note: The launched nixmac process environment is the source of truth for this run. launchctl getenv is diagnostic only and may be absent when the app is launched with an inline environment. Only environment variable names and presence checks are recorded; secret values are never written to the report.

processEnvVerification.note: The launched nixmac process environment is the source of truth for this run. launchctl getenv is diagnostic only and may be absent when the app is launched with an inline environment. Only environment variable names and presence checks are recorded; secret values are never written to the report.

waivers.label: Homebrew untracked package badge and add-to-config popover
waivers.reason: Not yet deterministic in the remote major-feature lane; needs a fixture with Homebrew installed and a known untracked tap/brew/cask diff before the suite can honestly claim coverage for the badge popover and Add to config action.

waivers.label: First-run permissions and nix setup/onboarding
waivers.reason: Current remote lane starts from a configured app state. This must become a dedicated clean-profile Computer Use scenario before the suite can honestly claim full main coverage.

waivers.label: nix-darwin sudo_local activation path
waivers.reason: The DXU remote lane disables sudo_local in the disposable fixture because unattended MacinCloud AppleScript activation can hang while creating /etc/pam.d/sudo_local. This is an explicit CI fixture waiver, not proof that the production sudo_local path works.

waivers.label: Preview indicator floating commit/revert UI
waivers.reason: Not yet in the remote major-feature lane; needs either a deterministic preview-state setup or a real workflow that activates it.

v2.canonicalSource: V2 is derived from the runner-owned scenarioLabels, scenarioGroups, scenarioProofCatalog, evolved case catalog, and coverage manifest until a later implementation consolidates those into a single registry module.

failureTaxonomy.app: The app UI/state did not behave as expected.
failureTaxonomy.provider: The real provider returned a billing, rate-limit, timeout, or model error.
failureTaxonomy.credential: A provider key was missing, invalid, unavailable, or not injected into the launched app process.
failureTaxonomy.remote_infra: DXU, SSH, launchd, app-server, macOS permissions, or remote activation infrastructure blocked the run.
failureTaxonomy.harness: Computer Use actions, artifact generation, report rendering, or runner bookkeeping failed.
failureTaxonomy.coverage: The suite lacks a scenario, manifest mapping, PR focus, or waiver for the behavior.
failureTaxonomy.inconclusive: The runner could not prove either pass or fail.

launch.label: App launches and first screen is usable
launch.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
launch.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
launch.proof: Accessibility text shows the nixmac window, Settings/History/Feedback controls, stepper, prompt text area, and disabled Send button.
launch.limitation: Does not prove provider or config state.

updateBanner.label: Update banner does not block the main workflow
updateBanner.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the assertion source and screenshots support human inspection.
updateBanner.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
updateBanner.proof: Runner checks for a visible Dismiss button. If present, it dismisses it; if absent, it proves no update banner blocked the initial UI.
updateBanner.limitation: The explicit dismiss interaction is only tested on runs where a banner is actually present.

settingsGeneral.label: Settings General tab visibly renders
settingsGeneral.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
settingsGeneral.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
settingsGeneral.proof: Computer Use clicked Settings and captured the General tab content.

settingsAIModels.label: Settings AI Models tab visibly renders
settingsAIModels.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
settingsAIModels.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
settingsAIModels.proof: Computer Use opened AI Models and captured provider/model/build controls.
settingsAIModels.limitation: Does not change models or verify every provider.

settingsAPIKeys.label: Settings API Keys tab visibly renders
settingsAPIKeys.evidenceStrengthReason: Pass relies on redacted accessibility text only, usually because screenshots are intentionally suppressed for a sensitive surface.
settingsAPIKeys.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim depends on accessibility text without a screenshot, usually due sensitive-surface redaction.
settingsAPIKeys.proof: Sensitive screenshot omitted; redacted accessibility text must show API Keys/OpenRouter/API-key controls.
settingsAPIKeys.limitation: Does not edit/delete keys and does not prove keychain persistence by itself.

settingsPreferences.label: Settings Preferences tab visibly renders
settingsPreferences.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
settingsPreferences.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
settingsPreferences.proof: Computer Use opened Preferences and captured confirmation controls.

history.label: My History opens and renders
history.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
history.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
history.proof: Computer Use opened History and captured a visible history/empty state.
history.limitation: Current run does not prove a newly saved change appears there.

console.label: Console opens and renders
console.evidenceStrengthReason: Pass relies on redacted accessibility text only, usually because screenshots are intentionally suppressed for a sensitive surface.
console.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim depends on accessibility text without a screenshot, usually due sensitive-surface redaction.
console.proof: Sensitive screenshot omitted; redacted accessibility text must show Console/log content.
console.limitation: Does not prove log completeness and may omit secret-bearing visuals by design.

feedback.label: Give Feedback opens and can be cancelled
feedback.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
feedback.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
feedback.proof: Computer Use opened and cancelled the feedback dialog.

reportIssue.label: Report Issue opens and can be cancelled
reportIssue.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
reportIssue.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
reportIssue.proof: Computer Use opened and cancelled the report issue dialog.

suggestionCards.label: Home suggestion cards are visible/clickable
suggestionCards.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
suggestionCards.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
suggestionCards.proof: Computer Use found and clicked a suggestion card; the UI stayed usable afterward.
suggestionCards.limitation: Does not prove every suggestion works.

typedIntent.label: A typed real intent can be submitted
typedIntent.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
typedIntent.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
typedIntent.proof: Prompt text appears in the Computer Use state after set_value.
typedIntent.limitation: Does not prove provider execution until Review is reached.

review.label: Real provider workflow reaches Review
review.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
review.failureClassReason: No more specific failure class matched.
review.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
review.proof: Polling reached Review-equivalent UI with Build & Test/Discard/Summary/Diff controls.

summary.label: Summary tab renders after intent review
summary.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
summary.failureClassReason: Provider-side failure wording was detected in the scenario notes.
summary.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
summary.proof: Summary text must mention the requested package/change domain.

diff.label: Diff tab renders after intent review
diff.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
diff.failureClassReason: Provider-side failure wording was detected in the scenario notes.
diff.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
diff.proof: Diff text must show the requested package/change in a configuration file.
diff.limitation: Does not prove the change builds or saves.

buildBoundary.label: Build & Test destructive boundary appears before activation
buildBoundary.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
buildBoundary.failureClassReason: Provider-side failure wording was detected in the scenario notes.
buildBoundary.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
buildBoundary.proof: Build & Test opens a confirmation dialog before activation; disposable runs confirm it and wait for Step 3.
buildBoundary.limitation: Without explicit disposable build-confirm mode, the runner still cancels at the boundary.

saveFlow.label: Step 3 Save / Keep changes persists a change
saveFlow.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
saveFlow.failureClassReason: Provider-side failure wording was detected in the scenario notes.
saveFlow.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
saveFlow.proof: In disposable build-confirm mode, Computer Use reaches Step 3, clicks Commit, and the runner verifies the disposable repo HEAD changed with a clean worktree.
saveFlow.limitation: When disposable build-confirm mode is not enabled, Save remains untested.

rollbackCleanup.label: Rollback cleanup returns disposable config to clean state
rollbackCleanup.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
rollbackCleanup.failureClassReason: Provider-side failure wording was detected in the scenario notes.
rollbackCleanup.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
rollbackCleanup.proof: After Save, Computer Use opens History, restores the pre-test baseline commit, and the runner verifies HEAD returned to that baseline with a clean worktree.
rollbackCleanup.limitation: Only runs when Save succeeded and a restorable disposable baseline exists.

discard.label: Discard confirmation is guarded and only confirmed in disposable state
discard.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
discard.failureClassReason: Provider-side failure wording was detected in the scenario notes.
discard.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
discard.proof: Discard opens a confirmation boundary when used. In full-lifecycle runs, the stronger History restore cleanup path can supersede Discard and is proven by rollback artifacts.
discard.limitation: When History restore cleanup passes, Discard itself is intentionally not clicked because the disposable config is already back at baseline.

visualCoverage.label: Core UX/UI surfaces are captured and inspectable
visualCoverage.evidenceStrengthReason: Accessibility text is the assertion source and screenshots support human inspection.
visualCoverage.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
visualCoverage.proof: Required core UI surfaces have screenshot and text artifacts.
visualCoverage.limitation: Does not prove screenshot annotations are exact bounding boxes.

visualProofQuality.label: Scenario results include inspectable visual/text evidence
visualProofQuality.evidenceStrengthReason: Pass relies on redacted accessibility text only, usually because screenshots are intentionally suppressed for a sensitive surface.
visualProofQuality.accessibilityRiskReason: The scenario is not primarily an accessibility-text assertion.
visualProofQuality.proof: Every passing scenario must have at least one primary screenshot or text artifact listed in the proof catalog.
visualProofQuality.limitation: Overlay callouts are visual aids; text snapshots remain the assertion source.

mainCoverageFreshness.label: Main branch user-visible coverage stays mapped
mainCoverageFreshness.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
mainCoverageFreshness.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
mainCoverageFreshness.proof: A repo-local coverage manifest maps major user-visible surfaces on main to Computer Use scenarios or explicit waivers, and the runner scans for unmapped candidate files.
mainCoverageFreshness.limitation: This proves scenario mapping freshness, not that every mapped scenario passed in the current run.

prSpecificCoverage.label: PR-specific user-visible behavior is covered when applicable
prSpecificCoverage.evidenceStrengthReason: Scenario did not pass, so no positive evidence strength is assigned.
prSpecificCoverage.failureClassReason: Provider-side failure wording was detected in the scenario notes.
prSpecificCoverage.accessibilityRiskReason: The claim has non-UI state or manifest proof in addition to UI evidence.
prSpecificCoverage.proof: Requires PR metadata and changed-file/user-visible focus input.
prSpecificCoverage.limitation: No PR-specific scenario is executed unless PR context is provided.

reportInspection.label: Generated HTML report is inspected with Computer Use
reportInspection.evidenceStrengthReason: Computer Use interacted with the real UI and matched expected state.
reportInspection.accessibilityRiskReason: Accessibility text is the semantic assertion source; screenshots are reviewer support.
reportInspection.proof: Computer Use opens the generated report on the remote Mac and sees report sections.
reportInspection.limitation: Does not prove a human reviewed every screenshot.
